children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: An order issued under this chapter shall contain the terms and conditions specified in this section.

children.heading: Establishment and membership of Board
children.label: b Establishment and membership of Board

children.heading: Producers
children.label: A Producers
children.text: Producers shall be appointed to the Board to represent States, with each State represented by the following number of members:Alabama1Alaska1Arizona1Arkansas1California5Colorado4Connecticut1Delaware1Florida1Georgia1Hawaii1Idaho2Illinois1Indiana1Iowa2Kansas1Kentucky1Louisiana1Maine1Maryland1Massachusetts1Michigan1Minnesota2Mississippi1Missouri1Montana5Nebraska1Nevada1New Hampshire1New Jersey1New Mexico2New York1North Carolina1North Dakota2Ohio1Oklahoma1Oregon2Pennsylvania1Rhode Island1South Carolina1South Dakota4Tennessee1Texas10Utah3Vermont1Virginia1Washington1West Virginia1Wisconsin1Wyoming5

children.heading: Feeders
children.label: B Feeders
children.text: The feeder sheep industry shall be represented on the Board by 10 members.

children.heading: Importers
children.label: C Importers
children.text: Importers shall be represented on the Board by 25 members.

children.heading: Alternates
children.label: D Alternates
children.text: The order shall provide that a unit represented by only 1 member may have an alternate member appointed to ensure representation at meetings of the Board.

children.heading: Producers
children.label: A Producers
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint producers to represent units established under paragraph (1)(A) from nominations submitted by eligible organizations certified under subsection (c)(3). An eligible organization may submit only nominations from the membership of the organization for the unit in which the organization is located. To be represented on the Board, each eligible organization shall submit to the Secretary at least 1.5 nominations for each appointment to the Board for which the unit is entitled to representation, as determined under paragraph (1)(A). If a unit is entitled to 1 appointment on the Board, the unit shall submit at least 2 nominations for the appointment.

children.heading: Feeders
children.label: B Feeders
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint representatives of the feeder sheep industry to seats established under paragraph (1)(B) from nominations submitted by qualified national organizations that represent the feeder sheep industry. To be represented on the Board, the industry shall provide at least 1.5 nominations for each appointment to the Board for which the feeder sheep industry is entitled to representation, as determined under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Importers
children.label: C Importers
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint importers to seats established under paragraph (1)(C) from nominations submitted by qualified organizations that represent importers, as determined by the Secretary. To be represented on the Board, importers shall provide at least 1.5 nominations for each appointment to the Board for which importers are entitled to representation, as determined under paragraph (1)(C).

children.heading: Producer nominations
children.label: A Producer nominations
children.text: The Secretary shall solicit nominations for each seat on the initially established Board to which a unit is entitled to representation from eligible organizations certified under paragraph (3). If no such organization exists in the unit, the Secretary shall solicit nominations for appointments in such manner as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: the active membership of the organization includes a significant number of feeders or importers in relation to the total membership of the organization;

children.label: ii
children.text: there is evidence of stability and permanency of the organization; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the organization has a primary and overriding interest in representing the feeder or importer segment of the sheep industry.

children.heading: Producer nominations
children.label: A Producer nominations
children.text: The solicitation of nominations for subsequent appointment to the Board from eligible organizations certified under paragraph (3) shall be initiated by the Secretary, with the Board securing the nominations for the Secretary.

children.heading: Feeder and importer nominations
children.label: B Feeder and importer nominations
children.text: The solicitation of feeder and importer nominations for seats on the Board shall be made by the Secretary in accordance with paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The eligibility of any organization to represent producers, and to participate in the making of nominations to represent producers under this section, shall be certified by the Secretary. The Secretary shall certify any organization that the Secretary determines meets the eligibility criteria established by the Secretary under this paragraph. An eligibility determination of the Secretary under this paragraph shall be final.

children.label: i
children.text: the geographic territory covered by the active membership of the organization;

children.label: ii
children.text: the nature and size of the active membership of the organization, including the proportion of the total number of active producers represented by the organization;

children.label: iii
children.text: evidence of stability and permanency of the organization;

children.label: iv
children.text: sources from which the operating funds of the organization are derived;

children.label: v
children.text: the functions of the organization; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the ability and willingness of the organization to further the aims and objectives of this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: the membership of the organization consists primarily of producers who own a substantial quantity of sheep; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an interest of the organization is in the production of sheep.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: 1 Terms
children.text: Each appointment to the Board shall be for a term of 3 years, except that appointments to the initially established Board shall be proportionately for 1-year, 2-year, and 3-year terms. No person may serve more than 2 consecutive 3-year terms, except that an elected officer of the Board shall not be subject to this sentence while the officer holds office.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 2 Compensation
children.text: A Board member shall serve without compensation, but shall be reimbursed for the reasonable expenses of the member incurred in performing the duties of the Board.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 3 Meetings
children.text: The order shall provide for at least an annual meeting of the Board and such additional meetings of the Board as may be required.

children.heading: Powers and duties of Board
children.label: e Powers and duties of Board

children.text: to elect officers of the Board, including a Chairperson, Vice Chairperson, and Secretary;
to administer the order in accordance with the terms and provisions of the order;
to recommend regulations to effectuate the terms and provisions of the order;
to elect members of the Board to serve on the Executive Committee;
to approve or reject budgets submitted by the Executive Committee;
on approval, to submit the budgets to the Secretary for the approval or disapproval of the Secretary;
to contract with entities, if necessary, to carry out plans or projects in accordance with this chapter;
to conduct programs of promotion, research, consumer information, education, industry information, and producer information;
to receive, investigate, and report to the Secretary complaints of violations of the order;
to recommend to the Secretary amendments to the order;
to provide the Secretary with prior notice of meetings of the Board to permit the Secretary, or a designated representative, to attend the meetings;

to provide, not less than annually, a report to producers, feeders, and importers accounting for funds expended by the Board and describing programs carried out under this chapter, and to make the report available to the public on request;
to establish 7 regions that, to the extent practicable, contain geographically contiguous States and approximately equal numbers of producers and sheep production;
to employ or retain necessary staff; and
to invest funds in accordance with subsection (k).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The order shall provide that the Board shall review budgets submitted by the Executive Committee, on a fiscal year basis, of anticipated expenses and disbursements by the Board, including probable costs of administration and promotion, research, consumer information, education, industry information, and producer information projects. On approval by the Board, the Board shall submit the budget to the Secretary for the approval of the Secretary.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: No expenditure of funds may be made by the Board unless the expenditure is authorized under a budget or budget amendment approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The order shall establish an Executive Committee to administer the terms and provisions of the order, as provided in this subsection, under the direction of the Board and consistent with the policies determined by the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: 7 members shall represent producers, with 1 member representing each of the regions established in the order;

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 member shall represent feeders; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 3 members shall represent importers; and

children.label: B
children.text: 3 members shall be the Chairperson, Vice Chairperson, and Secretary of the Board.

children.heading: Plans or projects
children.label: A Plans or projects
children.text: The Executive Committee shall develop plans or projects of promotion, research, consumer information, education, industry information, and producer information, which shall be paid for with assessments collected by the Board. The plans or projects shall not become effective until the plans or projects are approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Budgets
children.label: B Budgets
children.text: The Executive Committee shall be responsible for developing and submitting to the Board, for the approval of the Board, budgets, on a fiscal year basis, of the anticipated expenses and disbursements of the Board, including probable costs of promotion, research, consumer information, education, industry information, and producer information projects. The Board shall approve or disapprove a budget submitted by the Executive Committee, and, if approved, shall submit the budget to the Secretary for the approval of the Secretary.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: 4 Terms
children.text: A term of appointment to the Executive Committee shall be for 1 year.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 5 Chairperson
children.text: The Chairperson of the Board shall serve as Chairperson of the Executive Committee.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 6 Quorum
children.text: A quorum of the Executive Committee shall consist of 8 members.

children.heading: Expenses
children.label: 1 Expenses
children.text: The order shall provide that the Board shall be responsible for all expenses of the Board and the Executive Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: the contracting party shall develop and submit to the Board a plan or project, together with a budget or budgets that provides estimated costs to be incurred for the plan or project;

children.label: B
children.text: the plan or project, and the contract or agreement, shall not become effective until the plan or project has been approved by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: keep accurate records of all of the transactions of the party;

children.label: ii
children.text: account for funds received and expended, including staff time, salaries, and expenses expended on behalf of Board activities;

children.label: iii
children.text: make periodic reports to the Board of activities conducted; and

children.label: iv
children.text: make such other reports as the Board or the Secretary may require.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The order shall provide that each person making payment to a producer or feeder for sheep purchased from the producer or feeder shall, in the manner prescribed by the order, collect an assessment from the producer or feeder on each sheep sold by the producer or feeder.

children.heading: Processing
children.label: B Processing
children.text: Any person purchasing sheep for processing shall collect the assessment from the seller and remit the assessment to the Board in the manner prescribed by the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the rate of assessment under this paragraph shall be 1 cent per pound of live sheep sold.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The rate of assessment under this paragraph may be raised or lowered not more than ⁄ of a cent per pound in any 1 year, as recommended by the Executive Committee and approved by the Board and the Secretary, except that the rate of assessment under this paragraph shall not exceed 2.5 cents per pound of live sheep sold.15100

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The order shall provide that each person making payment to a producer, feeder, or handler of wool for wool purchased from the producer, feeder, or handler shall, in the manner prescribed by the order, collect an assessment on each pound of greasy wool sold.

children.heading: Processing
children.label: B Processing
children.text: Any person purchasing greasy wool for processing shall collect the assessment and remit the assessment to the Board in the manner prescribed by the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the rate of assessment under this paragraph shall be 2 cents per pound of greasy wool.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The rate of assessment under this paragraph may be raised or lowered not more than ⁄ of a cent per pound in any 1 year, as recommended by the Executive Committee and approved by the Board and the Secretary, except that the rate of assessment under this paragraph shall not exceed 4 cents per pound of greasy wool.210

children.label: A
children.text: pay an assessment on the sheep or sheep products at the time of sale at a rate equivalent to the rate provided for in paragraph (1) or (2), as appropriate; and

children.label: B
children.text: remit the assessment to the Board in the manner prescribed by the order.

children.label: A
children.text: pay the assessment on the sheep or greasy wool at the time of export at a rate equivalent to the rate provided for in paragraph (1) or (2), as appropriate; and

children.label: B
children.text: remit the assessment to the Board in the manner prescribed by the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The order shall provide that any person importing sheep or sheep product, and any person importing wool or products containing wool, into the United States shall pay an assessment to the Board in the manner prescribed by the order, except that this paragraph shall not apply to raw wool that is imported into the United States.

children.heading: Collection
children.label: B Collection
children.text: The Customs Service shall collect the assessment required under this paragraph and remit the assessment to the Secretary for disbursement to the Board.

children.heading: Rate for sheep and sheep products
children.label: C Rate for sheep and sheep products
I
children.text: in the case of a live sheep, 1 cent per pound; and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a sheep product, the equivalent of 1 cent per pound of live sheep, as determined by the Secretary in consultation with the domestic sheep industry.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The rate of assessment under this subparagraph may be raised or lowered not more than ⁄ cent per pound in any 1 year, as recommended by the Executive Committee and approved by the Board and the Secretary, except that the rate of assessment under this subparagraph shall not exceed 2.5 cents per pound.15100

children.heading: Rate for wool and wool products
children.label: D Rate for wool and wool products

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the rate of assessment under this paragraph for wool and products containing wool, shall be 2 cents per pound of degreased wool or the equivalent of degreased wool.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The rate of assessment under this subparagraph may be raised or lowered not more than ⁄ cent per pound in any 1 year, as recommended by the Executive Committee and approved by the Board and the Secretary, except that the rate of assessment under this subparagraph shall not exceed 4 cents per pound of degreased wool or the equivalent of degreased wool.210

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the order shall provide that 20 percent of the total assessments collected by the Board on the marketing of domestic sheep and domestic sheep products in any 1 year from a State shall be returned to the qualified State sheep board of the State.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: No qualified State sheep board shall receive less than $2,500 under subparagraph (A) in any year.

children.label: A
children.text: exclude certain imported materials or products that contain de minimis content levels of sheep or sheep products; and

children.label: B
children.text: waive the assessment due on the materials or products.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The order shall provide that assessments received by the Board shall be used by the Board for the payment of expenses incurred in administering the order, with authorization for a reasonable reserve.

children.heading: Reimbursement of Secretary
children.label: B Reimbursement of Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall be reimbursed for costs incurred in implementing and administering the order.

children.heading: Books and records of Board
children.label: j Books and records of Board
A
children.text: maintain such books and records as the Secretary may prescribe, which shall be available to the Secretary for inspection and audit;

children.label: B
children.text: prepare and submit to the Secretary, from time to time, such reports as the Secretary may prescribe; and

children.label: C
children.text: account for the receipt and disbursement of all funds entrusted to the Board.

children.heading: Audit
children.label: 2 Audit
children.text: The Board shall cause books and records of the Board related to the order to be audited by an independent auditor at the end of each fiscal year. The Board shall submit a report of the audit to the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: obligations of the United States or any agency of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: general obligations of any State or any political subdivision of a State;

children.label: C
children.text: any interest-bearing account or certificate of deposit of a bank that is a member of the Federal Reserve System; or

children.label: D
children.text: obligations fully guaranteed as to principal and interest by the United States.

children.heading: Use of income
children.label: 2 Use of income
children.text: Income from any investment under paragraph (1) may be used for any purpose for which the invested funds may be used.

children.heading: Prohibition on use of funds
children.label: l Prohibition on use of funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the order shall prohibit any funds collected by the Board under the order from being used in any manner for the purpose of influencing legislation or government action or policy.

children.label: A
children.text: the development and recommendation to the Secretary of amendments to the order; or

children.label: B
children.text: the communication to appropriate government officials, in response to a request made by the officials, of information relating to the conduct, implementation, or results of promotion, research, consumer information, education, industry information, or producer information activities under the order.

children.heading: False or misleading claims
children.label: 3 False or misleading claims
children.text: A plan or project conducted under this chapter shall not make false or misleading claims on behalf of sheep or sheep products or against a competing product.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The order shall require that each person making payment to a producer, feeder, or handler for sheep or sheep products, each importer and exporter of sheep or sheep products, and each person marketing sheep products of the person’s own production to maintain, and make available for inspection, such books and records as may be required by the order and file reports at the time, in the manner, and having the content prescribed by the order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Information from the records or reports shall be made available to the Secretary for the administration or enforcement of this chapter, or any order or regulation issued under this chapter.

children.heading: Other information
children.label: B Other information
children.text: The Secretary shall authorize the use under this chapter of information regarding persons paying producers, feeders, importers, handlers, or processors that is accumulated under a law or regulation other than this chapter or a regulation issued under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this chapter, all information obtained under paragraph (1) or (2) shall be kept confidential by all officers and employees of the Department and of the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary considers the information relevant;

children.label: ii
children.text: the information is revealed in a judicial proceeding or administrative hearing brought at the direction or on the request of the Secretary or to which the Secretary or any officer of the Department is a party; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the information relates to this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: the issuance of general statements, based on the reports, of the number of persons subject to an order or statistical data collected from the persons, which statements do not identify the information furnished by any person; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the publication, by direction of the Secretary, of the name of any person violating any order and a statement of the particular provisions of the order violated by the person.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: D Administration
children.text: No information obtained under this chapter may be made available to any agency or officer of the Federal Government for any purpose other than the implementation of this chapter or any investigatory or enforcement action necessary for the implementation of this chapter.

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: E Penalty
children.text: Any person who willfully violates this paragraph, on conviction, shall be subject to a fine of not more than $1,000 or to imprisonment for not more than 1 year, or both, and if the person is an officer or employee of the Board or the Department, shall be removed from office.

children.heading: Other terms and conditions
children.label: n Other terms and conditions
children.text: The order shall provide such terms and conditions, not inconsistent with this section, as are necessary to carry out the order, including provisions for the assessment of a penalty for the late payment of an assessment due under the order.
